overview: Iterative OpenCTI operations backlog that combines persona ownership, recurring intelligence routines, and Ralph Loop execution discipline.
goals: Convert OpenCTI intake into actionable daily outputs
Track persona-owned stories with clear status and audit trail
Improve the lab process over time without storing secrets in repo files
nonGoals: No plaintext secret storage in PRD, logs, or generated artifacts
No destructive OpenCTI modifications by default

operatingModel.opencti: System of record for CTI objects, relationships, and connector intake
operatingModel.personas: Analytical owners of each story and output
operatingModel.ralphLoop: One-story-per-iteration executor with progress, activity, and guardrails

authModel.recommended: Use one technical OpenCTI account and one API token at homelab stage
why: Persona ownership is tracked in backlog and report metadata

successMetrics: Daily outputs include confidence, caveats, and owner persona
Actionable hypotheses and prioritization notes are produced with clear relevance rationale
Backlog stories move through open/in_progress/done with evidence artifacts
openQuestions: Which OpenCTI objects should be auto-labeled vs manually curated?
What minimum signal threshold should trigger escalation for financial-sector workflows?

stories.title: Generate Daily Financial CTI Brief
stories.description: As Orion, I want a concise daily OpenCTI brief so financial-sector defenders can focus on high-value threats and avoid intake noise.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `outputs/opencti/daily-brief-YYYY-MM-DD.md` using `prompts/opencti-daily-brief.md`
Include 3-5 key points max, confidence statements, and caveats
Include a short watchlist for follow-up
Negative case: if useful signal is low, state this clearly instead of padding output

stories.title: Review KEV Priorities for Financial Context
stories.description: As Patch, I want to prioritize newly visible KEV/vulnerability content so remediation and monitoring teams can act quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `outputs/opencti/kev-prioritization-YYYY-MM-DD.md` from `prompts/opencti-kev-prioritization.md`
Each prioritized item includes relevance rationale and confidence
Include at least one monitoring or detection implication
Negative case: do not force high priority when context is weak

stories.title: Translate Intake into Financial Hunting Hypotheses
stories.description: As Raven, I want to transform recent OpenCTI content into practical hunt hypotheses for financial organizations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `outputs/opencti/financial-hunting-review-YYYY-MM-DD.md` using `prompts/opencti-financial-hunting-review.md`
Include 1-3 actionable hypotheses with suggested telemetry
Include detection opportunities and confidence/caveats
Include a detection logic starter section (Sigma plus at least one platform-specific draft when confidence is sufficient)
Negative case: reject generic commentary with no hunting action

stories.title: Assess Fraud-Relevant Phishing and Session Abuse Themes
stories.description: As Pulse, I want a fraud lens review of OpenCTI intake so ATO and phishing abuse patterns are tracked early.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `outputs/opencti/fraud-lens-review-YYYY-MM-DD.md`
Include top themes for phishing, AiTM, session hijacking, and ATO where present
Map each highlighted theme to business relevance
Negative case: avoid broad fraud claims without observable support

stories.title: Refine Daily Brief Presentation Template
stories.description: As Orion, I want a cleaner reusable daily brief layout so outputs are easier to consume and publish.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update `outputs/opencti/templates/daily-brief-layout.md`
Define mandatory sections, optional sections, and formatting rules
Add one filled sample section from a prior brief
Negative case: no layout that hides confidence/caveat fields

stories.title: Curate Source-Family Label Candidates
stories.description: As Orion, I want candidate source-family labels from recent intake so OpenCTI curation quality improves over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `outputs/opencti/source-family-label-candidates-YYYY-MM-DD.md`
For each candidate label, include reason and expected operational value
Identify labels that should remain manual-review only
Negative case: avoid label proposals without evidence

stories.title: Assess Feed Signal Quality
stories.description: As Orion, I want to score the current feed quality so we can tune connector usefulness and reduce low-value noise.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `outputs/opencti/feed-signal-quality-YYYY-MM-DD.md`
Assess at least OTX, URLhaus, KEV, and MITRE signal usefulness for financial-sector workflows
Include recommendations: keep, tune, or de-prioritize each source
Negative case: avoid scoring without clear criteria

stories.title: Translate Hunt Hypotheses into Detection Logic
stories.description: As Forge, I want high-confidence hunting hypotheses translated into reusable detection logic so the team can test and operationalize detections faster.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `outputs/opencti/detection-translation-YYYY-MM-DD.md` using `prompts/opencti-hunting-detection-translation.md`
Include at least one Sigma draft and one platform query draft (SPL, CQL, or Elastic KQL/EQL)
Search and document near matches from SigmaHQ, CrowdStrike LogScale, Splunk security_content, and Elastic detection-rules repositories
Include tuning guidance and a validation plan before production use
Negative case: avoid presenting unvalidated drafts as production-ready detections
